intro: Sclerolaena divaricata, also known as the spreading sandmat, is a perennial shrub native to Western Australia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Sclerolaena divaricata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
